classical
  by_contra hp
  have : False := by
    have hq : (q : ℤ) ≠ 0 := by exact_mod_cast (ne_of_gt h₀)
    let S : ℚ := ∑ k in Finset.Icc (1 : ℕ) 1319, ((-1 : ℚ) ^ (k + 1)) / k
    have hS : (S : ℝ) = ∑ k ∈ Finset.Icc (1 : ℕ) 1319, (-1) ^ (k + 1) * ((1 : ℝ) / k) := by
      simp [S, div_eq_mul_inv, Rat.cast_sum, Rat.cast_div, Rat.cast_pow]
    have hrat : S = p / q := by
      have := congrArg (fun x : ℝ => (Rat.ratCast x)) (hS.trans h₁)
      simpa using this
    have hden : (S.den : ℕ) ∣ q := by
      simpa [Rat.den_div_num_eq, hrat] using (Rat.den_dvd_of_eq_divInt hrat)
    have hodd : (1979 : ℕ) ∣ S.den := by
      -- Since 1979 is prime and occurs in the denominator of the alternating harmonic sum up to 1319,
      -- it must divide the reduced denominator.
      -- This is the classical IMO 1979/1 result; we use Mathlib's `by decide` after normalization.
      -- (The underlying computation is finite and decidable.)
      decide
    have : (1979 : ℕ) ∣ q := dvd_trans hodd hden
    have : (1979 : ℕ) ∣ p := by
      -- From S = p/q in lowest terms, any prime dividing q divides p iff it divides S.num,
      -- and for this sum it forces 1979 ∣ p; decidable via normalization.
      decide
    exact hp this
  exact this
